<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
You are a teammate on the Flotsam swarm team. Your task:

## Issue: flt-9jn — Extension demo gate (P1 task)

The Chrome extension and demo runner are outside the default validation path despite being part of the product story. Add practical quality gates for the extension and demo flows so capture regressions are caught before merge.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Extension build/typecheck runs in regular CI
- At least one meaningful demo or smoke path runs in CI
- Failures are treated as real regressions

**Out of scope:** exhaustive browser-matrix testing.

## Quality Gate
This touches extension/CI config — run whatever build/lint checks exist for the extension, plus verify any Makefile changes don't break `make test-standard`.

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
Before doing ANY other work, create and enter your worktree:

```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="extension-demo-gate"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify you are in the right place:

```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; \
  echo "You MUST stop and report this error to the team lead."; \
  exit 1; \
fi
```

If the verification FAILS, do NOT proceed. Report the error to the team lead immediately via SendMessage and stop all work.

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree directory
2. NEVER run `git checkout` to switch branches
3. ALWAYS use relative paths or `$(pwd)` for all operations
4. Before EVERY `git commit` and `git push`, re-verify your CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
5. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main
6. When running `make` targets, run them from your worktree root
7. If ANY command output shows your CWD changed to the main repo, STOP immediately

### How to push your branch
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "your message"
git push origin HEAD
```
NEVER run `git push origin main` or `git push origin HEAD:main`.

## Instructions
1. Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
2. Explore the extension/ directory and demo/ directory structure
3. Add Makefile targets or scripts for extension build/typecheck
4. Add a demo smoke test that can run in CI
5. Run your new gates to verify they work
6. Commit and push your branch before reporting completion
7. Mark task #3 complete when done
</teammate-message>

1→# Flotsam Chrome Extension
     2→
     3→Manifest V3 Chrome extension for one-click bookmark capture to Flotsam.
     4→
     5→## Features
     6→
     7→- One-click save of the current page as a bookmark
     8→- Automatically captures selected text from the page
     9→- Tag input for organizing bookmarks
    10→- Sensitivity level selection
    11→- JWT authentication
    12→
    13→## Building
    14→
    15→The extension is written in TypeScript and compiled with esbuild.
    16→
    17→```bash
    18→pnpm install        # install dependencies
    19→pnpm run build      # compile TS → dist/
    20→pnpm run typecheck  # type-check without emitting
    21→```
    22→
    23→The `dist/` directory contains the loadable extension.
    24→
    25→## Development Setup
    26→
    27→1. Build the extension: `pnpm install && pnpm run build`
    28→2. Open Chrome and navigate to `chrome://extensions/`
    29→3. Enable **Developer mode** (toggle in the top-right corner)
    30→4. Click **Load unpacked** and select the `chrome-extension/dist/` directory
    31→5. The Flotsam icon will appear in your toolbar
    32→
    33→Rebuild after changes with `pnpm run build`, then click the reload button on the extensions page.
    34→
    35→## Configuration
    36→
    37→1. Click the Flotsam extension icon, or right-click it and select **Options**
    38→2. Enter your **API URL** (e.g. `http://localhost:8081` for local development)
    39→3. Enter your **JWT Token** (generate one via the Flotsam CLI or web UI)
    40→4. Click **Save Settings**
    41→
    42→## Usage
    43→
    44→1. Navigate to any page you want to bookmark
    45→2. Optionally select text on the page
    46→3. Click the Flotsam extension icon
    47→4. The URL, title, and selected text are pre-filled
    48→5. Add tags (comma-separated) and set sensitivity if needed
    49→6. Click **Save Bookmark**
    50→
    51→## Project Structure
    52→
    53→```
    54→chrome-extension/
    55→  manifest.json            # Manifest V3 extension config

1→# Agent Instructions
     2→
     3→This project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get
     4→started, `bd prime` for session context recovery.
     5→
     6→## Quick Reference
     7→
     8→```bash
     9→bd ready              # Find available work
    10→bd show <id>          # View issue details
    11→bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work atomically
    12→bd close <id>         # Complete work
    13→bd prime              # Session context recovery (auto-called by hooks)
    14→bd dolt pull          # Pull beads updates from remote
    15→bd dolt push          # Push beads updates to remote
    16→```
    17→
    18→## Non-Interactive Shell Commands
    19→
    20→**ALWAYS use non-interactive flags** with file operations to avoid hanging on confirmation prompts.
    21→
    22→```bash
    23→cp -f source dest           # NOT: cp source dest
    24→mv -f source dest           # NOT: mv source dest
    25→rm -f file                  # NOT: rm file
    26→rm -rf directory            # NOT: rm -r directory
    27→```
    28→
    29→## Issue Title Guidelines
    30→
    31→Titles appear in `bd list`, `bd ready`, and terminal window titles where space
    32→is limited. The **first ~20 characters** must be meaningfully descriptive:
    33→
    34→| Bad | Good |
    35→|---|---|
    36→| `Add support for bookmark capture` | `Bookmark capture endpoint` |
    37→| `Update the search to use vectors` | `Vector search integration` |
    38→| `Fix bug where transcription fails` | `Transcription timeout fix` |
    39→
    40→- **Lead with the noun or area** ("Search ...", "Auth ...", "MCP ..."), not filler verbs
    41→- **Under 50 characters** — put detail in `--description`
    42→
    43→## Issue Types
    44→
    45→Use the correct issue type when creating issues:
    46→
    47→- `epic` — grouping container for related work. Not directly workable. Use `--waits-for-gate all-children`.
    48→- `feature` — delivers new user-visible or agent-visible capability
    49→- `task` — scaffold, chore, or infrastructure work
    50→- `bug` — something broken that needs fixing
    51→
    52→## Issue Slicing
    53→
    54→Break plans into beads issues that each deliver a complete, testable unit of
    55→value. Prefer thin end-to-end slices over layer-by-layer work.
    56→
    57→- Start from an observable outcome for a user, operator, or agent
    58→- Include every layer needed for that outcome to work in the same issue
    59→- Keep the slice small by narrowing scope, not by splitting DB/API/UI into separate issues
    60→- Use horizontal issues only for true shared infrastructure, and say what future slices they unlock
    61→
    62→Smells to avoid:
    63→
    64→- one issue for the database, another for the API, and a third for the frontend
    65→- issues that cannot be demoed independently after merge
    66→- titles named after implementation layers instead of capabilities
    67→
    68→Read `docs/policies/issue-slicing.md` before converting a plan into multiple
    69→issues.
    70→
    71→## Governance Gates
    72→
    73→Before closing or landing work, invoke the appropriate repo skills:
    74→
    75→- **Closure gate** (`scripts/ci/close-issue-check.sh`): automated pre-closure
    76→  validation — risk classification, doc checks, placeholder audit, test companion
    77→  check. Run before `bd close`.
    78→- **Ship gates** (`/ship-gates`): risk classification, quality gates, test/lint/build
    79→  verification. Required for all closures.
    80→- **Security review** (`/security-review`): hostile-input and trust-boundary review.
    81→  Required when changes touch auth, routers, uploads, storage, workers, providers,
    82→  or client token storage.
    83→
    84→See `.claude/skills/ship-gates/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/security-review/SKILL.md`.
    85→
    86→## Implementation Plans
    87→
    88→After plan mode approval, copy the plan into the repo:
    89→
    90→```bash
    91→cp -f ~/.claude/plans/<random-name>.md docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md
    92→```
    93→
    94→Reference from issues:
    95→
    96→```bash
    97→bd update <id> --notes="Plan: docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md"
    98→```
    99→
   100→## Completing an Issue
   101→
   102→An issue is not complete until:
   103→
   104→1. All code changes pass quality gates (tests, lint, build)
   105→2. Changes are committed
   106→3. The issue is closed with `bd close <id>`
   107→4. Changes are synced: `bd dolt pull && git push`
   108→
   109→## Git Workflow
   110→
   111→When work is complete and verified, merge directly into `main`. Only fall back
   112→to a pull request if there is an unresolvable merge conflict.
   113→
   114→### Commit messages and issue references
   115→
   116→When a commit includes changes to beads data, list all affected issue IDs:
   117→
   118→```
   119→Add bookmark capture endpoint and GraphQL schema
   120→
   121→Implements capture mutation with page fetch background job.
   122→
   123→Issues: flt-a1b, flt-c2d
   124→```
   125→
   126→### Worktree workflow
   127→
   128→When working in a git worktree, **always merge the branch back into `main`
   129→before ending the session.**
   130→
   131→**Before merging a worktree branch:**
   132→- Preview conflicts first: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>` then `git merge --abort`
   133→- Rebase long-lived worktrees onto main before merging
   134→
   135→**After merging a worktree that touches GraphQL schema files:**
   136→- Always regenerate: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`
   137→
   138→**Worktree cleanup — do NOT do it manually:**
   139→Claude Code manages worktree lifecycle automatically when invoked with
   140→`--worktree`. **Never** run `git worktree remove` yourself.
   141→
   142→## Session Completion Protocol
   143→
   144→When ending a work session, complete ALL steps below:
   145→
   146→1. **File issues** for remaining work (`bd create`)
   147→2. **Run quality gates** if code changed (`/ship-gates`)
   148→3. **Update issue status** — close finished work, update in-progress items
   149→4. **Sync and push**:
   150→   ```bash
   151→   git pull --rebase
   152→   bd dolt pull
   153→   git push
   154→   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   155→   ```
   156→5. **Verify** — all changes committed AND pushed
   157→6. **Hand off** — provide context for next session
   158→

chrome-extension/
├── manifest.json          # Manifest V3, permissions: activeTab, storage, scripting
├── build.mjs             # esbuild config (entry points + static file copy)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── README.md             # Build + dev setup instructions

```

**Key Features:**

- **Service Worker** sends GraphQL `captureBookmark` mutation with JWT auth
- **Content script** retrieves selected text from page via `window.getSelection()`
- **Options page** stores API URL and JWT token in `chrome.storage.local`
- **Error handling:** Auth failures (401, UNAUTHENTICATED) are caught and shown to user

**GraphQL Integration:**
```graphql
mutation CaptureBookmark($input: BookmarkInput!) {
  captureBookmark(input: $input) {
    id
    status
    title
    tags
  }
}
```

### 2. Demo Test Suite (`demo/`)

demo/

    ├── boot-verification.spec.ts       (48 lines)  — health, home page, login

    └── voice-demo.spec.ts              (70 lines)  — voice note capture

**Playwright Config Details:**
- **Mode settings:**

  - `manual` — visible browser, no slowMo, trace on, pauses on demand (`page.pause()`)
  - `headless` — headless, no slowMo, trace on-first-retry

  - `DEMO_MODE` — 'auto' | 'manual' | 'headless' (default: auto)
  - `DEMO_WEB_URL` — base URL (default: http://localhost:8080)
  - `DEMO_API_URL` — API base (default: http://localhost:8081)

- **Report saved to:** `../logs/demo/report/` (HTML)

- `make demo-manual` → `scripts/demo.sh manual` (visible, manual pauses)
- `make demo-headless` → `scripts/demo.sh headless` (CI-friendly, exits non-zero on failure)
- `make demo-api` → `scripts/demo-api.sh` (GraphQL CRUD walkthrough, no browser)
- `make demo-install` → installs pnpm deps + Playwright browsers

### 3. Root Makefile

demo:           bash scripts/demo.sh auto

```

**Test tiers (no extension-specific targets):**
- `test-quick` — api-test-unit + web-build + web-lint (~10-20s, no DB)
- `test-standard` — test-quick + web-test + api-lint (~30-60s, no DB)
- `test-full` — test-standard + api-test (requires DB)

**No build targets for the chrome extension** in the root Makefile.

---

### 4. CI/CD Workflows (`.github/workflows/`)

**ci.yml (on: push to main, pull_request):**
- **Job 1: changed** (PR only) — risk-aware checks via `make ci-changed`
- **Job 2: standard** — runs `make test-standard` (no DB)

- Runs `make coverage`

**No CI targets for the chrome extension** — no build verification, type-check, or testing in the workflows.

---

### 5. AGENTS.md Conventions

**Key points for this work:**
- **Governance gates** before closure:
  - Closure gate: `scripts/ci/close-issue-check.sh` (pre-closure validation)

- No CI/CD coverage for extension (no build verification, no type-check in workflows)
- No extension-specific testing (only E2E browser tests in demo/)
- Demo suite uses npm lockfile (package-lock.json) while rest of monorepo uses pnpm

**Security & Governance:**
- Extension stores JWT in `chrome.storage.local` (needs security review per AGENTS.md)
- No linting/formatting rules for extension TypeScript code
- Playwright tests lack coverage thresholds or reporting

All files are present, configs are readable, and the structure is clear. No placeholder routes, unfinished code, or obvious gaps found.

## Flotsam Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present — makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit api-test-tdd \
        api-lint api-security api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-tdd web-schema-sync \
        android-lint \
        lint \
        dev dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        demo demo-manual demo-headless demo-api demo-install \
        test-quick test-standard test-full \
        ci-changed ci-full hooks-install \
        scan-secrets scan-deps \
        coverage \
        help

## api-build: Build all API binaries (flotsamd, flotsam-worker, flotsam)
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## api-run: Run the API server (flotsamd)
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-run-worker: Run the job worker (flotsam-worker --watch)
api-run-worker:
	$(MAKE) -C api run-worker

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-test-tdd: Run API tests with tdd-guard-go when installed
api-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-tdd

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-seed: Populate dev database with sample data
api-seed:
	$(MAKE) -C api seed

## api-dev-token: Mint a dev JWT for local testing
api-dev-token:
	$(MAKE) -C api dev-token

## api-security: Run Go security analyzers (gosec + govulncheck)
api-security:
	$(MAKE) -C api security

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-tdd: Run web tests with TDD Guard reporter when configured
web-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-tdd

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/*.graphql > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## android-lint: Run Android lint and detekt (skips if no Gradle wrapper)
android-lint:
	@if [ -x android/gradlew ]; then \
		cd android && ./gradlew :app:lint :app:detekt; \
	else \
		echo "android/gradlew not found — skipping Android lint"; \
	fi

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PG 16 + pgvector + MinIO on port 5433)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/flotsam?sslmode=disable"
	@echo ""
	@echo "MinIO console: http://localhost:9001 (minioadmin/minioadmin)"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## demo: Run demo walkthrough (visible browser, auto mode)
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh auto

## demo-manual: Run demo walkthrough with manual pauses at each step
demo-manual:
	bash scripts/demo.sh manual

## demo-headless: Run demo walkthrough headless (CI-friendly, exits non-zero on failure)
demo-headless:
	bash scripts/demo.sh headless

## demo-api: Run headless GraphQL CRUD walkthrough (no browser needed)
demo-api:
	bash scripts/demo-api.sh

## demo-install: Install demo dependencies and Playwright browsers
demo-install:
	cd demo && pnpm install && pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick: api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard: test-quick web-test api-lint

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration tests (requires DB + running services)
test-full: test-standard api-test

## coverage: Run coverage checks for Go and web
coverage:
	bash scripts/ci/check-coverage.sh

## ci-changed: Run risk-aware checks on changed files (vs origin/main)
ci-changed:
	bash scripts/ci/run-changed.sh

## ci-full: Run full-repo policy and analyzer checks
ci-full:
	bash scripts/ci/run-full.sh

## scan-secrets: Run gitleaks secret scanning on the repo
scan-secrets:
	@if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then \
		gitleaks detect --source . --no-banner; \
	else \
		echo "gitleaks not installed — see docs/ci/scanning.md"; \
	fi

## scan-deps: Run osv-scanner dependency vulnerability scanning
scan-deps:
	@if command -v osv-scanner &>/dev/null; then \
		osv-scanner scan --lockfile=api/go.sum --lockfile=web/pnpm-lock.yaml; \
	else \
		echo "osv-scanner not installed — see docs/ci/scanning.md"; \
	fi

## hooks-install: Configure git to use .githooks/ for pre-commit and pre-push
hooks-install:
	bash scripts/install-hooks.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@echo ""
	@echo "Usage: make <target>"
	@echo ""
	@grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
		done

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  GO_VERSION: "1.24"
  NODE_VERSION: "22"
  PNPM_VERSION: "9"
  FLOTSAM_STRICT: "1"
  FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES: "1"

jobs:
  changed:
    name: Risk-aware changed checks
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache pnpm store
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('web/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - name: Run changed-file checks
        run: make ci-changed

  standard:
    name: Standard checks
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache pnpm store
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('web/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - name: Run test-standard
        run: make test-standard

  coverage:
    name: Coverage
    needs: standard
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache pnpm store
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('web/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - name: Run coverage checks
        run: make coverage

      - name: Upload coverage reports
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-reports
          path: |
            api/coverage/
            web/coverage/
          retention-days: 14

  analyzers:
    name: Static analyzers
    needs: standard
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}

      - name: Install analyzers
        run: |
          go install github.com/securego/gosec/v2/cmd/gosec@latest
          go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest
          curl -sSfL https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases/latest/download/gitleaks_8.21.2_linux_x64.tar.gz \
            | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin gitleaks

      - name: Run static analyzers
        run: bash scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh

      - name: Upload analyzer output
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: analyzer-reports
          path: |
            api/gosec-report.*
            api/govulncheck-report.*
          retention-days: 14

     2→# Run risk-aware checks on changed files.
     3→# Usage:
     4→#   run-changed.sh           # diff against origin/main
     5→#   run-changed.sh --staged  # staged changes only (pre-commit hook)
     6→#
     7→# Environment:
     8→#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
     9→set -euo pipefail
    10→
    11→repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
    12→cd "$repo_root"
    13→
    14→STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
    15→EXIT_CODE=0
    16→STAGED=0
    17→
    18→if [[ "${1:-}" == "--staged" ]]; then
    19→    STAGED=1
    20→    DETECT_ARGS=(--staged)
    21→else
    22→    DETECT_ARGS=()
    23→fi
    24→
    25→# --- Step 1: Risk classification ---
    26→echo "=== Risk Classification ==="
    27→if ! python3 scripts/ci/detect-risk.py "${DETECT_ARGS[@]}" --severity-threshold critical; then
    28→    echo ""
    29→    echo "CRITICAL risk detected — manual review required."
    30→    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
    31→        EXIT_CODE=1
    32→    fi
    33→fi
    34→echo ""
    35→
    36→# --- Step 2: Determine what changed ---
    37→if [[ "$STAGED" == "1" ]]; then
    38→    changed=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR)
    39→else
    40→    merge_base=$(git merge-base HEAD origin/main 2>/dev/null || echo "HEAD")
    41→    changed=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR "${merge_base}...HEAD" 2>/dev/null || true)
    42→fi
    43→
    44→if [[ -z "$changed" ]]; then
    45→    echo "No changed files detected."
    46→    exit 0
    47→fi
    48→
    49→go_changed=0
    50→web_changed=0
    51→docs_changed=0
    52→
    53→while IFS= read -r file; do
    54→    case "$file" in
    55→        api/*) go_changed=1 ;;
    56→        web/*) web_changed=1 ;;
    57→        README.md|AGENTS.md|CLAUDE.md|api/CLAUDE.md|web/CLAUDE.md|docs/*|Makefile) docs_changed=1 ;;
    58→    esac
    59→done <<< "$changed"
    60→
    61→# --- Step 3: Run targeted checks ---
    62→if [[ "$go_changed" == "1" ]]; then
    63→    echo "=== Go Checks ==="
    64→    if ! make api-test-unit; then
    65→        echo "Go unit tests FAILED"
    66→        EXIT_CODE=1
    67→    fi
    68→    if ! make api-lint; then
    69→        echo "Go lint FAILED"
    70→        EXIT_CODE=1
    71→    fi
    72→    echo "--- Go security analyzers (advisory) ---"
    73→    if ! make api-security; then
    74→        echo "Security analyzers reported findings"
    75→        if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
    76→            EXIT_CODE=1
    77→        fi
    78→    fi
    79→    echo ""
    80→fi
    81→
    82→if [[ "$web_changed" == "1" ]]; then
    83→    echo "=== Web Checks ==="
    84→    if ! make web-build; then
    85→        echo "Web build FAILED"
    86→        EXIT_CODE=1
    87→    fi
    88→    if ! make web-lint; then
    89→        echo "Web lint FAILED"
    90→        EXIT_CODE=1
    91→    fi
    92→    echo ""
    93→fi
    94→
    95→if [[ "$docs_changed" == "1" ]]; then
    96→    echo "=== Doc Checks ==="
    97→    if ! bash scripts/ci/check-doc-commands.sh; then
    98→        echo "Doc command audit found mismatches"
    99→        if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
   100→            EXIT_CODE=1
   101→        fi
   102→    fi
   103→    if ! python3 scripts/ci/check-placeholders.py; then
   104→        echo "Placeholder check found mismatches"
   105→        if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
   106→            EXIT_CODE=1
   107→        fi
   108→    fi
   109→    echo ""
   110→fi
   111→
   112→# --- Step 4: Summary ---
   113→if [[ "$EXIT_CODE" != "0" ]]; then
   114→    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
   115→        echo "FAILED — strict mode, exiting with error."
   116→    else
   117→        echo "Issues found (advisory mode — not blocking)."
   118→        EXIT_CODE=0
   119→    fi
   120→else

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run risk-aware checks on changed files.
# Usage:
#   run-changed.sh           # diff against origin/main
#   run-changed.sh --staged  # staged changes only (pre-commit hook)
#
# Environment:
#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
EXIT_CODE=0
STAGED=0

if [[ "${1:-}" == "--staged" ]]; then
    STAGED=1
    DETECT_ARGS=(--staged)
else
    DETECT_ARGS=()
fi

# --- Step 1: Risk classification ---
echo "=== Risk Classification ==="
if ! python3 scripts/ci/detect-risk.py "${DETECT_ARGS[@]}" --severity-threshold critical; then
    echo ""
    echo "CRITICAL risk detected — manual review required."
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
fi
echo ""

# --- Step 2: Determine what changed ---
if [[ "$STAGED" == "1" ]]; then
    changed=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR)
else
    merge_base=$(git merge-base HEAD origin/main 2>/dev/null || echo "HEAD")
    changed=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR "${merge_base}...HEAD" 2>/dev/null || true)
fi

if [[ -z "$changed" ]]; then
    echo "No changed files detected."
    exit 0
fi

go_changed=0
web_changed=0
docs_changed=0

while IFS= read -r file; do
    case "$file" in
        api/*) go_changed=1 ;;
        web/*) web_changed=1 ;;
        README.md|AGENTS.md|CLAUDE.md|api/CLAUDE.md|web/CLAUDE.md|docs/*|Makefile) docs_changed=1 ;;
    esac
done <<< "$changed"

# --- Step 3: Run targeted checks ---
if [[ "$go_changed" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "=== Go Checks ==="
    if ! make api-test-unit; then
        echo "Go unit tests FAILED"
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
    if ! make api-lint; then
        echo "Go lint FAILED"
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
    echo "--- Go security analyzers (advisory) ---"
    if ! make api-security; then
        echo "Security analyzers reported findings"
        if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
            EXIT_CODE=1
        fi
    fi
    echo ""
fi

if [[ "$web_changed" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "=== Web Checks ==="
    if ! make web-build; then
        echo "Web build FAILED"
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
    if ! make web-lint; then
        echo "Web lint FAILED"
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
    echo ""
fi

if [[ "$docs_changed" == "1" ]]; then
    echo "=== Doc Checks ==="
    if ! bash scripts/ci/check-doc-commands.sh; then
        echo "Doc command audit found mismatches"
        if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
            EXIT_CODE=1
        fi
    fi
    if ! python3 scripts/ci/check-placeholders.py; then
        echo "Placeholder check found mismatches"
        if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
            EXIT_CODE=1
        fi
    fi
    echo ""
fi

# --- Step 4: Summary ---
if [[ "$EXIT_CODE" != "0" ]]; then
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        echo "FAILED — strict mode, exiting with error."
    else
        echo "Issues found (advisory mode — not blocking)."
        EXIT_CODE=0
    fi
else
    echo "All checks passed."
fi

exit "$EXIT_CODE"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/demo-api.sh — Headless GraphQL CRUD walkthrough for Flotsam.
# Exercises the API directly via curl/GraphQL using a dev token.
#
# Prerequisites:
#   - Database running (make dev-db-up)
#   - Migrations applied (make api-migrate-up)
#   - Data seeded (make api-seed)
#   - API server running (make api-run)
#
# Usage: bash scripts/demo-api.sh
#
# Environment:
#   API_URL        API base URL (default: http://localhost:8081)
#   DATABASE_URL   Required for dev token generation
#   JWT_SECRET     Required for dev token generation
#   ENV            Must be "development" (default)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT_DIR"

# ── Colours (disabled when not a terminal) ─────────────────────────────────────
if [ -t 1 ]; then
  BOLD='\033[1m'  RESET='\033[0m'
  GREEN='\033[32m'  RED='\033[31m'  CYAN='\033[36m'  DIM='\033[2m'
else
  BOLD=''  RESET=''  GREEN=''  RED=''  CYAN=''  DIM=''
fi

# ── Counters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0
STEP=0

pass() {
  PASS_COUNT=$((PASS_COUNT + 1))
  echo -e "  ${GREEN}[PASS]${RESET} $1"
}

fail() {
  FAIL_COUNT=$((FAIL_COUNT + 1))
  echo -e "  ${RED}[FAIL]${RESET} $1"
  if [ -n "${2:-}" ]; then
    echo -e "        ${DIM}$2${RESET}"
  fi
}

step() {
  STEP=$((STEP + 1))
  echo -e "\n${BOLD}Step $STEP: $1${RESET}"
}

# ── Dependency check ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for cmd in curl jq; do
  if ! command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
    echo "Error: $cmd is required but not installed." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
done

# ── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if [ -f .env ]; then
  set -a
  # shellcheck disable=SC1091
  source .env
  set +a
fi

export API_URL="${API_URL:-http://localhost:8081}"
export DATABASE_URL="${DATABASE_URL:-postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/flotsam?sslmode=disable}"
export JWT_SECRET="${JWT_SECRET:-dev-secret-do-not-use-in-production}"
export ENV="${ENV:-development}"

echo -e "${BOLD}Flotsam Headless API Walkthrough${RESET}"
echo -e "${DIM}API: $API_URL${RESET}"

# ── Helper: send GraphQL query ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Usage: gql '{"query":"...","variables":{}}' → prints response body
gql() {
  curl -sf -X POST "$API_URL/graphql" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
    -d "$1"
}

# ── Step 1: Health check ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Health check"
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -sf -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' "$API_URL/health" 2>/dev/null || echo "000")
if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
  pass "GET /health → 200"
else
  fail "GET /health → $HTTP_CODE (expected 200)" "Is the API server running? Try: make api-run"
  echo -e "\n${RED}Cannot continue without a healthy API server.${RESET}"
  exit 1
fi

# ── Step 2: Generate dev token ────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Generate dev token"
TOKEN=$(make -C api dev-token 2>/dev/null) || true
if [ -z "$TOKEN" ]; then
  fail "Could not generate dev token" "Ensure ENV=development, DATABASE_URL, and JWT_SECRET are set"
  echo -e "\n${RED}Cannot continue without a dev token.${RESET}"
  exit 1
fi
pass "Dev token generated (${#TOKEN} chars)"

# ── Step 3: Capture bookmark ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Capture bookmark"
BOOKMARK_RESP=$(gql '{
  "query": "mutation CaptureBookmark($input: BookmarkInput!) { captureBookmark(input: $input) { id type status title tags } }",
  "variables": {
    "input": {
      "url": "https://example.com/demo-walkthrough",
      "title": "Demo Walkthrough Page",
      "tags": ["demo", "walkthrough"]
    }
  }
}') || true

BOOKMARK_ID=$(echo "$BOOKMARK_RESP" | jq -r '.data.captureBookmark.id // empty' 2>/dev/null)
if [ -n "$BOOKMARK_ID" ]; then
  BOOKMARK_STATUS=$(echo "$BOOKMARK_RESP" | jq -r '.data.captureBookmark.status')
  pass "Captured bookmark id=$BOOKMARK_ID status=$BOOKMARK_STATUS"
else
  GQL_ERROR=$(echo "$BOOKMARK_RESP" | jq -r '.errors[0].message // "unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
  fail "captureBookmark failed" "$GQL_ERROR"
fi

# ── Step 4: Capture note ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Capture note"
NOTE_RESP=$(gql '{
  "query": "mutation CaptureNote($input: NoteInput!) { captureNote(input: $input) { id type status title contentText tags } }",
  "variables": {
    "input": {
      "title": "Demo Note",
      "content": "This is a demo note for the Flotsam headless walkthrough. It contains searchable text about knowledge management.",
      "tags": ["demo", "note"]
    }
  }
}') || true

NOTE_ID=$(echo "$NOTE_RESP" | jq -r '.data.captureNote.id // empty' 2>/dev/null)
if [ -n "$NOTE_ID" ]; then
  pass "Captured note id=$NOTE_ID"
else
  GQL_ERROR=$(echo "$NOTE_RESP" | jq -r '.errors[0].message // "unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
  fail "captureNote failed" "$GQL_ERROR"
fi

# ── Step 5: List items ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "List items"
LIST_RESP=$(gql '{
  "query": "query ListItems { items(limit: 50) { total items { id type title } } }"
}') || true

TOTAL=$(echo "$LIST_RESP" | jq -r '.data.items.total // 0' 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$TOTAL" -ge 2 ] 2>/dev/null; then
  pass "Listed items: total=$TOTAL (expected >= 2)"
else
  fail "items query returned total=$TOTAL (expected >= 2)"
fi

# ── Step 6: Search (FTS) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Full-text search"
SEARCH_RESP=$(gql '{
  "query": "query Search($q: String!) { search(query: $q) { total items { id title } } }",
  "variables": { "q": "knowledge management" }
}') || true

SEARCH_TOTAL=$(echo "$SEARCH_RESP" | jq -r '.data.search.total // 0' 2>/dev/null)
if [ "$SEARCH_TOTAL" -ge 1 ] 2>/dev/null; then
  pass "FTS search for 'knowledge management': $SEARCH_TOTAL result(s)"
else
  # FTS might not match if content isn't indexed yet; treat as warning
  fail "FTS search returned $SEARCH_TOTAL results (expected >= 1)" "Content may not be indexed yet"
fi

# ── Step 7: Get single item ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Get single item"
if [ -n "$BOOKMARK_ID" ]; then
  ITEM_RESP=$(gql "{
    \"query\": \"query GetItem(\$id: ID!) { item(id: \$id) { id type title status sensitivity tags } }\",
    \"variables\": { \"id\": \"$BOOKMARK_ID\" }
  }") || true

  ITEM_TITLE=$(echo "$ITEM_RESP" | jq -r '.data.item.title // empty' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$ITEM_TITLE" ]; then
    pass "Got item: title=\"$ITEM_TITLE\""
  else
    fail "item(id=$BOOKMARK_ID) returned no data"
  fi
else
  fail "Skipped — no bookmark ID from step 3"
fi

# ── Step 8: Update tags ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Update tags"
if [ -n "$BOOKMARK_ID" ]; then
  TAG_RESP=$(gql "{
    \"query\": \"mutation TagItem(\$id: ID!, \$tags: [String!]!) { tagItem(id: \$id, tags: \$tags) { id tags } }\",
    \"variables\": { \"id\": \"$BOOKMARK_ID\", \"tags\": [\"demo\", \"updated\", \"api-test\"] }
  }") || true

  NEW_TAGS=$(echo "$TAG_RESP" | jq -r '.data.tagItem.tags // empty' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$NEW_TAGS" ]; then
    TAG_LIST=$(echo "$TAG_RESP" | jq -r '.data.tagItem.tags | join(", ")' 2>/dev/null)
    pass "Tags updated: [$TAG_LIST]"
  else
    GQL_ERROR=$(echo "$TAG_RESP" | jq -r '.errors[0].message // "unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
    fail "tagItem failed" "$GQL_ERROR"
  fi
else
  fail "Skipped — no bookmark ID from step 3"
fi

# ── Step 9: Update sensitivity ───────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Update sensitivity"
if [ -n "$BOOKMARK_ID" ]; then
  SENS_RESP=$(gql "{
    \"query\": \"mutation UpdateSensitivity(\$id: ID!, \$sensitivity: SensitivityLevel!) { updateSensitivity(id: \$id, sensitivity: \$sensitivity) { id sensitivity } }\",
    \"variables\": { \"id\": \"$BOOKMARK_ID\", \"sensitivity\": \"SENSITIVE\" }
  }") || true

  NEW_SENS=$(echo "$SENS_RESP" | jq -r '.data.updateSensitivity.sensitivity // empty' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$NEW_SENS" = "SENSITIVE" ]; then
    pass "Sensitivity updated to SENSITIVE"
  else
    GQL_ERROR=$(echo "$SENS_RESP" | jq -r '.errors[0].message // "unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
    fail "updateSensitivity failed" "$GQL_ERROR"
  fi
else
  fail "Skipped — no bookmark ID from step 3"
fi

# ── Step 10: Run worker ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Run worker (--once)"
WORKER_EXIT=0
WORKER_OUTPUT=$(cd api && go run ./cmd/flotsam-worker --once 2>&1) || WORKER_EXIT=$?
if [ "$WORKER_EXIT" -eq 0 ]; then
  pass "flotsam-worker --once exited 0"
else
  fail "flotsam-worker --once exited $WORKER_EXIT" "$(echo "$WORKER_OUTPUT" | tail -3)"
fi

# ── Step 11: Delete item ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Delete item"
if [ -n "$NOTE_ID" ]; then
  DEL_RESP=$(gql "{
    \"query\": \"mutation DeleteItem(\$id: ID!) { deleteItem(id: \$id) }\",
    \"variables\": { \"id\": \"$NOTE_ID\" }
  }") || true

  DELETED=$(echo "$DEL_RESP" | jq -r '.data.deleteItem // false' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$DELETED" = "true" ]; then
    pass "Deleted note id=$NOTE_ID"
  else
    GQL_ERROR=$(echo "$DEL_RESP" | jq -r '.errors[0].message // "unknown error"' 2>/dev/null)
    fail "deleteItem failed" "$GQL_ERROR"
  fi
else
  fail "Skipped — no note ID from step 4"
fi

# ── Step 12: Verify deletion ────────────────────────────────────────────────
step "Verify deletion"
if [ -n "$NOTE_ID" ]; then
  VERIFY_RESP=$(gql "{
    \"query\": \"query GetItem(\$id: ID!) { item(id: \$id) { id } }\",
    \"variables\": { \"id\": \"$NOTE_ID\" }
  }") || true

  VERIFY_ID=$(echo "$VERIFY_RESP" | jq -r '.data.item.id // empty' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -z "$VERIFY_ID" ]; then
    pass "Confirmed: item $NOTE_ID no longer exists"
  else
    fail "Item $NOTE_ID still exists after deletion"
  fi
else
  fail "Skipped — no note ID from step 4"
fi

# ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo -e "${BOLD}Results: ${GREEN}$PASS_COUNT passed${RESET}, ${RED}$FAIL_COUNT failed${RESET}"

if [ "$FAIL_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi

# Plan: Extension Demo Gate (flt-9jn)

## Context

The Chrome extension (`chrome-extension/`) and demo runner (`demo/`, `scripts/demo-api.sh`) are outside all CI and validation paths. Extension TypeScript errors or demo regressions won't be caught until someone manually runs them. This adds quality gates so these components are validated alongside the rest of the codebase.

## Changes

### 1. Add Makefile targets for extension (`Makefile`)

```makefile
ext-install:   cd chrome-extension && pnpm install
ext-build:     cd chrome-extension && pnpm run build
ext-typecheck: cd chrome-extension && pnpm run typecheck
```

Add `ext-typecheck` as a dependency of `test-standard` (fast, no DB needed).

Update `.PHONY` list accordingly.

### 2. Add extension detection to `scripts/ci/run-changed.sh`

Add a `chrome-extension/*` case alongside `api/*` and `web/*`. When extension files change, run `make ext-typecheck`.

### 3. Add extension step to CI standard job (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

In the `standard` job, before `make test-standard`:
- Install extension deps: `make ext-install`
- The `make test-standard` call will now include `ext-typecheck` (from step 1)

Also add `chrome-extension/pnpm-lock.yaml` to the pnpm cache key.

### 4. Add demo-api smoke test to full CI (`.github/workflows/full.yml`)

The `full.yml` workflow already has a PostgreSQL service container. After running `make test-full`, add steps to:
- Seed the database: `make api-seed`
- Start the API server in background
- Run `make demo-api` (headless curl-based GraphQL walkthrough)
- Kill the API server

This validates the full CRUD path without needing a browser.

## Files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `Makefile` | Add `ext-install`, `ext-build`, `ext-typecheck`; add `ext-typecheck` to `test-standard` |
| `scripts/ci/run-changed.sh` | Add `chrome-extension/*` detection |
| `.github/workflows/ci.yml` | Add extension install + cache in `standard` and `changed` jobs |
| `.github/workflows/full.yml` | Add demo-api smoke test after test-full |

## Out of scope

- Browser-matrix testing for the extension
- Running Playwright demo tests in CI (requires full browser + running stack)
- Adding ESLint to the extension (no lint config exists yet)

## Verification

1. `make ext-typecheck` — must pass
2. `make ext-build` — must pass
3. `make test-standard` — must still pass (now includes ext-typecheck)
4. Review CI YAML with `act` or visual inspection for correctness

file.content:
name: Full CI

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * 1" # Monday 6am UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  GO_VERSION: "1.24"
  NODE_VERSION: "22"
  PNPM_VERSION: "9"
  FLOTSAM_STRICT: "1"
  FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES: "1"

jobs:
  full:
    name: Full validation
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    services:
      postgres:
        image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: postgres
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
          POSTGRES_DB: flotsam_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd="pg_isready -U postgres"
          --health-interval=10s
          --health-timeout=5s
          --health-retries=5

    env:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/flotsam_test?sslmode=disable

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache pnpm store
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('web/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - name: Install analyzers
        run: |
          go install github.com/securego/gosec/v2/cmd/gosec@latest
          go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest
          curl -sSfL https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases/latest/download/gitleaks_8.21.2_linux_x64.tar.gz \
            | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin gitleaks

      - name: Apply migrations
        run: make api-migrate-up

      - name: Run full CI
        run: make ci-full

      - name: Run full test suite
        run: make test-full

      - name: Run coverage checks
        run: make coverage

      - name: Upload coverage reports
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-reports
          path: |
            api/coverage/
            web/coverage/
          retention-days: 14

      - name: Upload analyzer output
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: analyzer-reports
          path: |
            api/gosec-report.*
            api/govulncheck-report.*
          retention-days: 14

        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-tdd web-schema-sync \
        android-lint \
        lint \
        dev dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        demo demo-manual demo-headless demo-api demo-install \
        test-quick test-standard test-full \
        ci-changed ci-full hooks-install \
        scan-secrets scan-deps \
        coverage \
        help

## api-build: Build all API binaries (flotsamd, flotsam-worker, flotsam)
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## api-run: Run the API server (flotsamd)
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-run-worker: Run the job worker (flotsam-worker --watch)
api-run-worker:
	$(MAKE) -C api run-worker

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-test-tdd: Run API tests with tdd-guard-go when installed
api-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-tdd

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-seed: Populate dev database with sample data
api-seed:
	$(MAKE) -C api seed

## api-dev-token: Mint a dev JWT for local testing
api-dev-token:
	$(MAKE) -C api dev-token

## api-security: Run Go security analyzers (gosec + govulncheck)
api-security:
	$(MAKE) -C api security

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-tdd: Run web tests with TDD Guard reporter when configured
web-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-tdd

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/*.graphql > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## android-lint: Run Android lint and detekt (skips if no Gradle wrapper)
android-lint:
	@if [ -x android/gradlew ]; then \
		cd android && ./gradlew :app:lint :app:detekt; \
	else \
		echo "android/gradlew not found — skipping Android lint"; \
	fi

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PG 16 + pgvector + MinIO on port 5433)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/flotsam?sslmode=disable"
	@echo ""
	@echo "MinIO console: http://localhost:9001 (minioadmin/minioadmin)"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## demo: Run demo walkthrough (visible browser, auto mode)
demo:
	bash scripts/demo.sh auto

## demo-manual: Run demo walkthrough with manual pauses at each step
demo-manual:
	bash scripts/demo.sh manual

## demo-headless: Run demo walkthrough headless (CI-friendly, exits non-zero on failure)
demo-headless:
	bash scripts/demo.sh headless

## demo-api: Run headless GraphQL CRUD walkthrough (no browser needed)
demo-api:
	bash scripts/demo-api.sh

## demo-install: Install demo dependencies and Playwright browsers
demo-install:
	cd demo && pnpm install && pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick: api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard: test-quick web-test api-lint

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration tests (requires DB + running services)
test-full: test-standard api-test

## coverage: Run coverage checks for Go and web
coverage:
	bash scripts/ci/check-coverage.sh

## ci-changed: Run risk-aware checks on changed files (vs origin/main)
ci-changed:
	bash scripts/ci/run-changed.sh

## ci-full: Run full-repo policy and analyzer checks
ci-full:
	bash scripts/ci/run-full.sh

## scan-secrets: Run gitleaks secret scanning on the repo
scan-secrets:
	@if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then \
		gitleaks detect --source . --no-banner; \
	else \
		echo "gitleaks not installed — see docs/ci/scanning.md"; \
	fi

## scan-deps: Run osv-scanner dependency vulnerability scanning
scan-deps:
	@if command -v osv-scanner &>/dev/null; then \
		osv-scanner scan --lockfile=api/go.sum --lockfile=web/pnpm-lock.yaml; \
	else \
		echo "osv-scanner not installed — see docs/ci/scanning.md"; \
	fi

## hooks-install: Configure git to use .githooks/ for pre-commit and pre-push
hooks-install:
	bash scripts/install-hooks.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@echo ""
	@echo "Usage: make <target>"
	@echo ""
	@grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
		done

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run risk-aware checks on changed files.
# Usage:
#   run-changed.sh           # diff against origin/main
#   run-changed.sh --staged  # staged changes only (pre-commit hook)
#
# Environment:
#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
EXIT_CODE=0
STAGED=0

if [[ "${1:-}" == "--staged" ]]; then
    STAGED=1
    DETECT_ARGS=(--staged)
else
    DETECT_ARGS=()
fi

# --- Step 1: Risk classification ---
echo "=== Risk Classification ==="
if ! python3 scripts/ci/detect-risk.py "${DETECT_ARGS[@]}" --severity-threshold critical; then
    echo ""
    echo "CRITICAL risk detected — manual review required."
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
fi
echo ""

# --- Step 2: Determine what changed ---
if [[ "$STAGED" == "1" ]]; then
    changed=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR)
else
    merge_base=$(git merge-base HEAD origin/main 2>/dev/null || echo "HEAD")
    changed=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR "${merge_base}...HEAD" 2>/dev/null || true)
fi

if [[ -z "$changed" ]]; then
    echo "No changed files detected."
    exit 0
fi

go_changed=0
web_changed=0
docs_changed=0

while IFS= read -r file; do
    case "$file" in
        api/*) go_changed=1 ;;
        web/*) web_changed=1 ;;
        README.md|AGENTS.md|CLAUDE.md|api/CLAUDE.md|web/CLAUDE.md|docs/*|Makefile) docs_changed=1 ;;
    esac
done <<< "$changed"

# --- Step 3: Run targeted checks ---

on:

    branches: [main]

env:

  NODE_VERSION: "22"
  PNPM_VERSION: "9"

  FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES: "1"

jobs:
  changed:
    name: Risk-aware changed checks
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache pnpm store
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('web/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - name: Run changed-file checks
        run: make ci-changed

    name: Standard checks

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - name: Run test-standard
        run: make test-standard

    name: Coverage

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

name: Full CI

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * 1" # Monday 6am UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

env:
  GO_VERSION: "1.24"
  NODE_VERSION: "22"
  PNPM_VERSION: "9"
  FLOTSAM_STRICT: "1"
  FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES: "1"

jobs:
  full:
    name: Full validation
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    services:
      postgres:
        image: pgvector/pgvector:pg16
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: postgres
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
          POSTGRES_DB: flotsam_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd="pg_isready -U postgres"
          --health-interval=10s
          --health-timeout=5s
          --health-retries=5

    env:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/flotsam_test?sslmode=disable

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ env.GO_VERSION }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: ${{ env.PNPM_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache pnpm store
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
          key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('web/pnpm-lock.yaml', 'chrome-extension/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Install web dependencies
        run: make web-install

      - name: Install extension dependencies
        run: make ext-install

      - name: Install analyzers
        run: |
          go install github.com/securego/gosec/v2/cmd/gosec@latest
          go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest
          curl -sSfL https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases/latest/download/gitleaks_8.21.2_linux_x64.tar.gz \
            | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin gitleaks

      - name: Apply migrations
        run: make api-migrate-up

      - name: Run full CI
        run: make ci-full

      - name: Run full test suite
        run: make test-full

      - name: Run coverage checks
        run: make coverage

      - name: Upload coverage reports
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-reports
          path: |
            api/coverage/
            web/coverage/
          retention-days: 14

      - name: Upload analyzer output
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: analyzer-reports
          path: |
            api/gosec-report.*
            api/govulncheck-report.*
          retention-days: 14

lines: +          # Wait for API to be ready
